questions.question: Does handleMessage switch have an explicit case 'error': branch before the default case?
evidence_required: grep output showing case 'error': exists in handleMessage
questions.pass_criteria: case 'error': exists as a case in the handleMessage switch, before default:

questions.question: Does case 'error' delegate to handleErrorMessage(payload), and does handleErrorMessage delegate fatal=true to handleFatalError?
questions.actionable_check: Read src-tauri/ui/main.ts lines around case 'error' and handleErrorMessage function
evidence_required: code snippet showing case 'error' calls handleErrorMessage
code snippet showing handleErrorMessage checks payload.fatal and calls handleFatalError for fatal=true
questions.pass_criteria: case 'error' calls handleErrorMessage(msg.payload as ErrorPayload); handleErrorMessage calls handleFatalError(payload.message) when payload.fatal is true

questions.question: Is ErrorPayload type defined in types.ts with message and fatal fields?
evidence_required: grep output showing ErrorPayload interface exists
questions.pass_criteria: ErrorPayload interface has message: string and fatal: boolean fields; ErrorMessage type exists in SidecarMessage union

questions.question: Is ErrorPayload imported in main.ts?
evidence_required: grep output showing ErrorPayload in import statement
questions.pass_criteria: ErrorPayload appears in the import from './types' at the top of main.ts

questions.question: Does handleErrorMessage handle non-fatal errors with a transient notification?
questions.actionable_check: Read handleErrorMessage function body in main.ts
evidence_required: code snippet showing non-fatal path uses loadingPrompt and setTimeout for 3s display
questions.pass_criteria: When payload.fatal is false, function displays error in loadingPrompt element with 3s auto-clear via setTimeout
